items.briefing:
Australian organisations are facing growing pressure around AI use in the workplace, with new reports highlighting that many businesses have lost visibility into how employees are actually using AI tools — creating security and compliance gaps. Separately, Australian banks have been flagged for lagging behind on DMARC email protections, meaning staff at those organisations may be more exposed to email spoofing and phishing attacks that impersonate their bank.

A China-linked threat group is also actively targeting critical systems across the Asia-Pacific region, which is a timely reminder for Australian organisations to review access controls and monitor for unusual network activity.

**Practical tip:** Ask your IT team whether your organisation has visibility over which AI tools staff are using — unsanctioned apps can introduce real data and security risks.

featured.title: Australian banks lag on strict DMARC email protection
featured.quote: Australian banks' weak email security leaves customers vulnerable to preventable phishing attacks.
